title: euosma callosa
intro: Euosma callosa, commonly known as the Indian spikenard, is a flowering plant species in the family Asclepiadaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing fever and aiding in wound healing. The therapeutic actions of Euosma callosa include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its ability to soothe and protect the body. The bioactive constituents of Euosma callosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and therapeutic actions.
side_effects: Euosma callosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications. Ingestion of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact may lead to redness, itching, and irritation. The plant's alkaloids may also exacerbate conditions like hypertension, anxiety, and insomnia, and in rare cases, it can trigger seizures or coma. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of Euosma callosa may lead to liver damage and other systemic problems.
